---
title: "Database Schema"
---

## DuckDB Table Structure

Reference schema for jobs and candidates tables.

---

## Jobs Table

### Definition

```sql
CREATE TABLE jobs (
    job_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    
    -- Basic Info
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    company TEXT,
    location TEXT,
    
    -- Job Details
    employment_type TEXT,  -- full-time, part-time, contract
    experience_level TEXT, -- entry, mid, senior, executive
    description TEXT,
    
    -- Compensation
    salary_min DECIMAL,
    salary_max DECIMAL,
    
    -- Skills (Arrays)
    required_skills TEXT[],
    preferred_skills TEXT[],
    
    -- Metadata
    posted_date DATE,
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'active',  -- active, closed, on-hold
    source TEXT,  -- linkedin, indeed, internal, referral
    external_url TEXT,
    
    -- Timestamps
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

### Field Descriptions

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | INTEGER | ✅ | Unique identifier |
| `title` | TEXT | ✅ | Job title (e.g., "Senior Software Engineer") |
| `company` | TEXT | | Company name |
| `location` | TEXT | | City, State or "Remote" |
| `employment_type` | TEXT | | full-time, part-time, contract |
| `experience_level` | TEXT | | entry, mid, senior, executive |
| `description` | TEXT | | Full job description |
| `salary_min` | DECIMAL | | Minimum salary/range start |
| `salary_max` | DECIMAL | | Maximum salary/range end |
| `required_skills` | TEXT[] | | **Critical** - skills candidate must have |
| `preferred_skills` | TEXT[] | | Nice-to-have skills |
| `posted_date` | DATE | | When job was posted |
| `status` | TEXT | | active jobs are shown, others hidden |
| `source` | TEXT | | Where job was posted |
| `external_url` | TEXT | | Link to apply |

### Example Data

```sql
INSERT INTO jobs VALUES (
    1,                              -- job_id
    'Senior Software Engineer',     -- title
    'TechCorp Inc',                 -- company
    'Remote',                       -- location
    'full-time',                    -- employment_type
    'senior',                       -- experience_level
    'We are looking for...',        -- description
    140000,                         -- salary_min
    180000,                         -- salary_max
    ['Python', 'AWS', 'Docker'],   -- required_skills
    ['Kubernetes', 'React'],        -- preferred_skills
    '2026-05-01',                   -- posted_date
    'active',                       -- status
    'linkedin',                     -- source
    'https://example.com/jobs/1',  -- external_url
    NOW(),                          -- created_at
    NOW()                           -- updated_at
);
```

---

## Candidates Table

### Definition

```sql
CREATE TABLE candidates (
    candidate_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    
    -- Personal Info
    first_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    last_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    email TEXT,
    phone TEXT,
    location TEXT,
    
    -- Current Role
    current_title TEXT,
    current_company TEXT,
    
    -- Experience
    years_experience INTEGER,
    experience_level TEXT,  -- entry, mid, senior, executive
    
    -- Skills (Array)
    skills TEXT[],
    
    -- Education
    education_level TEXT,   -- high-school, bachelor, master, phd
    education_field TEXT,
    
    -- Logistics
    availability TEXT,      -- immediate, 2-weeks, 1-month, not-looking
    salary_expectation_min DECIMAL,
    salary_expectation_max DECIMAL,
    
    -- Links
    resume_url TEXT,
    linkedin_url TEXT,
    portfolio_url TEXT,
    
    -- Source
    source TEXT,            -- applied, referred, sourced, agency
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'active',  -- active, placed, archived
    
    -- Timestamps
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

### Field Descriptions

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `candidate_id` | INTEGER | ✅ | Unique identifier |
| `first_name` | TEXT | ✅ | First name |
| `last_name` | TEXT | ✅ | Last name |
| `email` | TEXT | | Email address |
| `phone` | TEXT | | Phone number |
| `location` | TEXT | | City, State |
| `current_title` | TEXT | | Current job title |
| `current_company` | TEXT | | Current employer |
| `years_experience` | INTEGER | | Total years of experience |
| `experience_level` | TEXT | | entry, mid, senior, executive |
| `skills` | TEXT[] | | **Critical** - all candidate skills |
| `education_level` | TEXT | | Highest degree |
| `education_field` | TEXT | | Field of study |
| `availability` | TEXT | | When can start |
| `salary_expectation_min` | DECIMAL | | Minimum expected salary |
| `salary_expectation_max` | DECIMAL | | Maximum expected salary |
| `resume_url` | TEXT | | Link to resume |
| `linkedin_url` | TEXT | | LinkedIn profile URL |
| `portfolio_url` | TEXT | | GitHub/portfolio URL |
| `source` | TEXT | | How candidate was found |
| `status` | TEXT | | active candidates shown in matching |

### Example Data

```sql
INSERT INTO candidates VALUES (
    1,                              -- candidate_id
    'Jane',                         -- first_name
    'Doe',                          -- last_name
    'jane@email.com',               -- email
    '555-123-4567',                 -- phone
    'New York, NY',                 -- location
    'Senior Developer',             -- current_title
    'StartupXYZ',                   -- current_company
    8,                              -- years_experience
    'senior',                       -- experience_level
    ['Python', 'AWS', 'Docker', 'React'],  -- skills
    'master',                       -- education_level
    'Computer Science',             -- education_field
    '2-weeks',                      -- availability
    150000,                         -- salary_expectation_min
    170000,                         -- salary_expectation_max
    'https://example.com/resume.pdf',  -- resume_url
    'https://linkedin.com/in/janedoe', -- linkedin_url
    'https://github.com/janedoe',   -- portfolio_url
    'sourced',                      -- source
    'active',                       -- status
    NOW(),                          -- created_at
    NOW()                           -- updated_at
);
```

---

## Optional Tables

### Candidate Experience (Detailed Work History)

```sql
CREATE TABLE candidate_experience (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    candidate_id INTEGER REFERENCES candidates(candidate_id),
    company TEXT,
    title TEXT,
    start_date DATE,
    end_date DATE,
    is_current BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    description TEXT,
    skills_used TEXT[]
);
```

### Match Scores (Pre-computed)

```sql
CREATE TABLE match_scores (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    candidate_id INTEGER REFERENCES candidates(candidate_id),
    job_id INTEGER REFERENCES jobs(job_id),
    overall_score DECIMAL,
    skills_match_score DECIMAL,
    experience_match_score DECIMAL,
    level_match_score DECIMAL,
    location_match_score DECIMAL,
    salary_match_score DECIMAL,
    matched_skills TEXT[],
    missing_skills TEXT[],
    computed_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

### Notes (If Using Backend)

```sql
CREATE TABLE candidate_notes (
    note_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    candidate_id INTEGER REFERENCES candidates(candidate_id),
    job_id INTEGER REFERENCES jobs(job_id),
    user_email TEXT,
    note_text TEXT NOT NULL,
    rating INTEGER CHECK (rating BETWEEN 1 AND 5),
    tags TEXT[],
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

---

## Indexes for Performance

```sql
-- For skills array searches
CREATE INDEX idx_candidates_skills ON candidates USING GIN (skills);
CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_skills ON jobs USING GIN (required_skills);

-- For level filtering
CREATE INDEX idx_candidates_level ON candidates(experience_level);
CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_level ON jobs(experience_level);

-- For match score lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_match_scores_candidate ON match_scores(candidate_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_match_scores_job ON match_scores(job_id);
```

---

## Data Quality Guidelines

### Required Fields for Matching

For best results, ensure these fields are populated:

**Jobs:**
- ✅ `required_skills` - At least 3-5 skills
- ✅ `experience_level` - entry/mid/senior/executive
- ✅ `status` - Set to 'active' for visible jobs
- ✅ `salary_min`/`salary_max` - For salary matching
- ✅ `location` - Include "Remote" if applicable

**Candidates:**
- ✅ `skills` - Comprehensive list (10+ recommended)
- ✅ `experience_level` - Matches job levels
- ✅ `years_experience` - Numeric value
- ✅ `availability` - For filtering
- ✅ `status` - Set to 'active' for available candidates

### Common Issues

**Skills not matching:**
- Check spelling consistency (Python vs python)
- Ensure both job and candidate have skills populated
- Verify skills are arrays, not comma-separated strings

**Experience level mismatches:**
- Use consistent values: entry, mid, senior, executive
- Don't use variations like "junior" or "staff"

**Location not working:**
- Use "Remote" explicitly for remote jobs
- Be consistent with city/state format

---

## Migration from Other Systems

### From CSV

```python
import duckdb
import pandas as pd

# Read CSV
df = pd.read_csv('candidates.csv')

# Connect to DuckDB
conn = duckdb.connect('candidates.db')

# Create table and insert
conn.execute("CREATE TABLE candidates AS SELECT * FROM df")
```

### From PostgreSQL

```bash
# Export from Postgres
pg_dump -t candidates old_db > candidates.sql

# Import to DuckDB
duckdb candidates.db < candidates.sql
```

### From ATS Systems

Most ATS systems can export to CSV. Map fields:

| ATS Field | DuckDB Field |
|-----------|--------------|
| First Name | first_name |
| Last Name | last_name |
| Email | email |
| Current Position | current_title |
| Years of Experience | years_experience |
| Skills | skills (as array) |

---

## Next Steps

- [Data Export](data-export.qmd) - Weekly refresh process
- [Matching Algorithm](matching-algorithm.qmd) - How data is used
- [Installation](installation.qmd) - Set up your database
